etymology.description: Depicts rope wrapped around two pieces of wood to keep them straight -> later borrowed phonetically to mean 'no'
etymology.interpretation: The character 弗 originally depicted a rope wrapped around two sticks, an image that suggests containment or prevention. This visual was phonetically borrowed to express 'no', leveraging the form for its sound rather than its visual logic.

examples.chinese: 他对此毫无兴趣(他對此毫無興趣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì cǐ háo wú xìngqù.
examples.english: He has no interest in this.

examples.chinese: 事情并非如我所愿(事情並非如我所願)。
examples.pinyin: Shìqíng bìng fēi rú wǒ suǒ yuàn.
examples.english: Things are not as I wished.

examples.chinese: 这并不简单(這並不簡單)。
